model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the CLICK ME button
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (361,205) said dx=50 dy=160 conf=1.00 -> (411,365)
  iter 1: at (411,365) said dx=90 dy=-70 conf=0.80 -> (501,295)
  iter 2: at (456,330) said dx=-70 dy=-43 conf=1.00 -> (386,287)
  iter 3: at (438,319) said dx=-48 dy=-21 conf=0.90 -> (390,298)
  iter 4: at (426,314) said dx=-31 dy=-18 conf=0.90 -> (396,296)
  iter 5: at (419,310) said dx=-45 dy=0 conf=0.90 -> (374,310)
  iter 6: at (408,310) said dx=0 dy=-7 conf=0.90 -> (408,302)
  iter 7: at (408,308) said dx=-33 dy=-2 conf=0.90 -> (374,306)
  iter 8: at (399,307) said dx=0 dy=0 conf=1.00 -> (399,307)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to act as a DVOC verifier to refine the click position on a "CLICK ME" button.
The red crosshair is the current predicted click position.
The target is the "CLICK ME" button (green area).
Looking at the image:
- Red crosshair is exactly in the center of the "CLICK ME" button and the blue reference circle.
- The crosshair is centered on the button.

Therefore, the offset is (0, 0) with high confidence.
